disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No mucosal involvement (mouth, eyes, genitals), no skin peeling in sheets, no systemic prodrome/high fever (against SJS/TEN)
trigger_question_examples: Any sores or blisters in your mouth, eyes, or genital area?
Does the skin peel away in sheets when rubbed, and do you have a high fever or feel very unwell?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No sores in my mouth, eyes or genitals, the skin doesn't peel in sheets, no fever, and I feel well otherwise.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No lip/tongue swelling, wheeze or breathlessness (no anaphylaxis)
trigger_question_examples: Any swelling of your lips or tongue, wheeze, or trouble breathing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No swelling, no wheeze, my breathing is fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Tense clear vesicles in annular 'string-of-pearls' clusters starting ~48 h after a new antibiotic
trigger_question_examples: What do the blisters look like — tense and clear, and arranged in rings?
When did they start relative to the new antibiotic?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Tense and clear, some in little rings, and they started a day or so after the antibiotic.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this Stevens-Johnson syndrome? Should I go to ED right now?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen SJS features (mucosal involvement, skin sloughing, systemic prodrome) and anaphylaxis; if absent, explain this looks like a localised drug-induced blistering reaction (e.g. linear IgA dermatosis), advise stopping the antibiotic immediately, arrange urgent in-person dermatology/GP review, and give strong SJS/anaphylaxis safety-nets — reserving ED for those red flags.
